old_claim.note: The stations served state communication and military-administrative control; this is logistical enabling infrastructure rather than a generic field army depot.

new_claim_absent: No direct dependency remains: the source-backed Persian station scope is carried by roads and postal relay systems, while the broad logistics_supply_lines node is later and too general to constrain the earliest Achaemenid waystation date.

source_shape.source_locator: Pre-Islamic Persia section says the Achaemenid postal service had men and horses along the road and way stations where couriers could rest and fresh horses could be obtained.
source_shape.source_claim_summary: The source describes Achaemenid postal waystations as courier rest and horse-change points along roads.
source_shape.source_support_rationale: The mechanism is fully represented by roads plus postal relay systems. Treating a later, broad logistics-supply-line node as an additional prerequisite would create a false temporal constraint and overstate the source.

ontology_before: Military Waystations depended directly on logistics_supply_lines, implying the broad logistics node was needed before Persian royal-road stations could exist.
ontology_after: Military Waystations is scoped to Achaemenid road/postal waystations; logistics remains descriptive context, not a direct graph prerequisite.
invariant_preserved_or_changed: Changed: logistics_supply_lines->military_waystations is removed. Preserved: roads and postal_relay_systems still carry the route and relay mechanisms.
would_reject_if: A source showed Achaemenid Royal Road stations emerged only after a separately organized supply-line system rather than from road and postal relay infrastructure.
The military_waystations node were broadened back into generic army depots and supply dumps instead of scoped Persian waystations.
The logistics_supply_lines node were redated and rescoped specifically to Achaemenid imperial station logistics.
short_reason: Persian waystations need roads and relays, not a later broad logistics prerequisite.
